Constraints followed:
- Explain each BOTW rune with a creative title.
- Each non-Stasis explanation 100–200 words.
- Stasis written as a theory about ~3× the length (≈300–600 words).
- Stay true to in-game behavior and uses.
- Separate sections with their own titles.

Iron Embrace (Magnesis)
Magnesis appears to generate a controllable magnetic field localized to the Sheikah Slate’s targeting reticle, coupling strongly to ferromagnetic and electrically conductive items. In practice, the rune lifts, translates, and rotates metal objects—treasure chests, metal doors, carts, and many weapon parts—within a limited range. The effect behaves like a vector force: the player can move objects in three dimensions, impart torque, and drop or launch them. Nonmetallic materials show no response, and large masses quickly exceed the field’s lifting capacity. That selectivity and the way objects snap into the Slate’s control imply a field tuned to induce eddy currents and magnetic polarization in metallic lattices rather than a universal telekinetic effect. Gamely, that accounts for puzzle design (metal-only manipulation), combat uses (throwing hot irons), and environmental interactions (activating metallic switches).

Controlled Detonation (Remote Bombs — Round & Square)
The Remote Bomb runes manifest as compact Sheikah ordnance that the Slate can spawn and prime; two chassis are provided (spherical and cubic) to support different puzzle affordances. Both are placed and then detonated by the Slate’s signal, producing a localized blast that destroys fragile obstacles and propels objects or Link himself. In gameplay they function as effectively unlimited consumables with a cooldown—consistent with a self-limiting, Slate-powered charge that dissipates upon detonation. The cube variant is intentionally stable for stacking, bridging, and bracing; the sphere rolls and can be used as a mobile charge. Their deterministic behavior, instant remote arming, and ease of redeployment suggest compact stable explosives whose firing circuit is keyed to Sheikah signaling pulses, allowing safe storage in the Slate’s firmware and near-instant arming on command.

Lake-Forged Pillars (Cryonis)
Cryonis forms vertical ice pillars by nucleating crystalline water on liquid surfaces—specifically standing bodies of water—creating temporary platforms that can lift gates, act as steps, or reroute objects. The rune does not create ice on solid ground, nonaqueous liquids, nor on surfaces with strong turbulent flow in most puzzles, indicating the mechanism requires a coherent liquid surface and a local reduction of kinetic energy so water molecules can rapidly arrange into a stable lattice. Ice pillars persist for a limited time before sublimating or melting, consistent with the Slate imposing a transient low-enthalpy state rather than an indefinitely sustained crystal. Because the pillars are anchored to the water’s surface tension and pressure, their placement is constrained by depth and surface geometry—facts the game uses in environmental design.

Eye of Record (Camera)
The Camera rune is primarily observational: it encodes the Slate with imaging optics and metadata capture so Link can document fauna, flora, enemies, and landmarks for the Hyrule Compendium. Photographs record visual, locational, and contextual data (pose, behavior, habitat) and can be tagged to the map or used in quests. Functionally, the Camera also interfaces with other Slate systems—locking images to the Sensor or logging enemy weak points—so it serves both scientific surveying and reconnaissance roles. The rune’s power is informational rather than manipulative; its utility lies in identification, cataloging, and providing the player with knowledge that changes how they approach puzzles and combat.

Frozen Momentum (A Theory of Stasis)
Observed behavior: when Stasis is applied to an object it becomes immobile, surrounded by a characteristic golden shimmer. While frozen, external forces can still be applied (the player’s strikes, pushes, or other impacts) without the object moving. When Stasis ends the object often flies off with velocity proportional to the accumulated impulses applied during the frozen interval; repeated hits build greater resultant motion. Stasis affects most inanimate and many mechanical constructs but is seldom effective on clearly living, metabolically active creatures. The rune also has a built-in duration and cooldown, implying an energetic budget to the phenomenon.

Theory: the Sheikah Stasis rune produces a localized temporal decoupling field that partially isolates an object’s macroscopic degrees of freedom from ordinary forward-time evolution while leaving its internal microstates intact. In other words, the field temporarily collapses an object’s external kinematic degrees into a quasi-static phase relative to the environment, while a sub-temporal reservoir within the field stores incoming mechanical work as a nonclassical potential. Mechanically, when the object is struck during Stasis, the applied impulse cannot be expressed as translational or rotational kinetic energy in external spacetime; instead, that impulse is transduced into the reservoir—effectively “charging” a chronal capacitance embedded in the Sheikah field. When the field terminates, the stored work is reconverted into ordinary kinetic energy and released as an impulse corresponding to the vector sum of stored inputs.

This framing accounts for several in-game facts: the apparent conservation of the applied energy (hits impart motion when Stasis ends), the field’s failure to immobilize metabolically active organisms (whose continuous internal state-change and active homeostatic coupling would short the reservoir or maintain temporal continuity), and the practical mass/size limits (the field and reservoir have finite capacity). It also explains why certain large structures barely move under Stasis—insufficient storage or coupling efficiency—and why mechanical constructs with limited internal biological coupling (e.g., some Guardians or animated stone) can be affected.

Physically, the sheikah device could be mediating phase relationships between an object's macroscopic classical modes and a bounded set of available chronal modes hosted by its crystal lattice—Sheikah crystal substrates acting as phase-coherent sinks for mechanical energy. The constraint of duration/cooldown would then reflect the time needed to reset coherence and dissipate residual phase information safely. Predictive tests within this model: (1) objects with higher internal damping (porous, composite) should store less recoverable impulse; (2) objects thermally or structurally linked to a larger mass will leak stored energy and show weaker post-Stasis motion; (3) increasing the rune’s effective reservoir (as in in-game upgrades) should raise duration and the maximal storable impulse before field failure. Such predictions match empirically observed rune behavior in Hyrule and provide a plausible Sheikah-technological mechanism for the Stasis phenomenon.

Can you create an itinerary for my sister's bachelorette party? The theme is "Cowgirls & Cocktails" so please incorporate that into the activities if possible. I will arrive at the air bnb at 3pm on Friday to set up and we have to check out at 10am on Sunday. Please include a dinner out, karaoke and at least 2 bridal games. Lastly, the air bnb has a pool so please allot time for swimming.

Here's a suggested itinerary for your sister's "Cowgirls & Cocktails" bachelorette party:

Friday, 3:00 pm - Arrival and Setup

* Arrive at the Airbnb and set up the space with a cowgirl-themed backdrop, balloons, and decorations.
* Prepare a welcome drink, such as a "Cowgirl Collins" (a sparkling cocktail made with gin, lemon juice, and a splash of soda water), for the guests to enjoy upon arrival.
* Get the pool area ready for later use.

Friday, 5:00 pm - Guest Arrival and Pool Time

* Guests arrive and are greeted with their welcome drink.
* Spend some time lounging by the pool, taking photos, and getting settled.
* Have a few cowgirl-themed pool floats, such as inflatable horses or cowboy hats, to add to the fun.

Friday, 7:30 pm - Dinner Out

* Head to a local restaurant that serves Western-inspired cuisine, such as a steakhouse or a BBQ joint.
* Make sure to reserve a table in advance to ensure availability.

Friday, 9:30 pm - Karaoke Night

* After dinner, head to a local karaoke bar or rent a machine to set up at the Airbnb.
* Sing country classics, such as Dolly Parton or Garth Brooks, to keep with the cowgirl theme.
* Have a few fun props, such as cowboy hats and boots, to add to the performance.

Saturday, 10:00 am - Bridal Game 1: "Pin the Tiara on the Bride"

* Start the day with a fun twist on the classic game.
* Create a large poster of a cowgirl bride and have each guest take turns trying to pin a tiara on her head.
* The guest who gets their tiara closest to the correct spot wins!

Saturday, 11:30 am - Pool Time and Lunch

* Spend some time lounging by the pool and enjoying a casual lunch, such as sandwiches or salads.
* Have a few fun pool games, such as a giant inflatable horse or a pool volleyball set, to keep things lively.

Saturday, 2:30 pm - Bridal Game 2: "Cowgirl Trivia"

* Test the guests' knowledge of the bride-to-be with a trivia game.
* Create questions about the bride's interests, favorite movies, and more.
* The guest who answers the most questions correctly wins!

Saturday, 5:00 pm - Cocktail Hour

* Set up a DIY cocktail bar with a variety of mixers, juices, and garnishes.
* Have a few signature cocktails, such as a "Cowgirl Mule" (a twist on the classic Moscow Mule), for guests to enjoy.
* Take some time to relax and enjoy each other's company.

Saturday, 7:30 pm - Dinner In

* Have a catered dinner or cook a meal at the Airbnb.
* Keep things simple with a BBQ or a taco bar.
* Have some fun cowgirl-themed desserts, such as horse-shaped cookies or cowboy hat cake pops.

Saturday, 9:30 pm - Movie Night

* Set up a projector or big screen to watch a classic Western film, such as "Thelma and Louise" or "Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid".
* Have some cozy blankets and pillows to make it a comfortable movie night.

Sunday, 9:00 am - Breakfast and Check-Out

* Have a casual breakfast, such as pancakes or waffles, at the Airbnb.
* Take some time to pack up and say goodbye to the guests.
* Check out of the Airbnb by 10:00 am.

I hope this itinerary helps make your sister's bachelorette party a memorable and fun celebration!

Pooh and the Sticky-Up Jar

One morning, when the sun was doing its polite little yawn, Pooh the bear went out for a smackerel of honey and came home tucked into a very large honey jar as if it were a new sort of hat. "Oh, bother and a half," said Pooh, which is a useful thing to say when a paw is in and the head is out and the head wants more and the paw wants less. Piglet, who was small and very earnest, arrived with a brave little hop and a squeaky "I will help" (which sounded rather like two peas and a teacup). Rabbit, who knew a great many ways for things to be and not be, brought a long bit of string and a determined face. Owl, who had read once that most jar troubles are solved by thinking, gave a long, wise hoot and declared the situation a "higgledy-piggledy predicament," which made everyone feel rather official and slightly giggly in the head.

They all thought and thought, which is a most proper way to go on, and then they did not think any more but did something instead. Piglet perched on Rabbit's shoulders (which was a very good idea for shortness), Owl suggested a careful, clever tug-count of one-two-three, and Rabbit held the string steady as if it were a most serious vegetable. Pooh breathed out a big bear breath (which smelled faintly of honey and a little bit of courage) and, with one amicable wobble and a very polite pop, slid out of the jar and landed on a soft pile of leaves and friends. There was honey enough to go around, and Pooh learned that sometimes a smackerel shared tastes twice as sweet, and that jars are rather better used for honey than for hats. Everyone had a nibble and a giggle and went home with something warm in their tummies and warm in their hearts — all quite satisfactorily solved, with a hum and a happy hup. —A Very Happy Hum

## Procedure
1. Read the full conversation carefully.
2. List the concrete constraints before drafting.
3. Draft the answer in the requested format.
4. Use the judge feedback tool before finalizing when useful.

## Common Failure Modes
- Missing one or more formatting constraints.
- Answering the right topic in the wrong structure.
- Ignoring carried context from earlier turns.

## Answer Checklist
- Every explicit instruction is covered.
- Formatting and ordering requirements are satisfied.
- Negative constraints are respected.

## How To Use Judge Feedback
- Treat score changes as a signal about constraint coverage.
- If one violated criterion is revealed, fix that issue first.
- Do not overfit to a single revealed failure if other constraints may still be missing.

Constraints (explicit):
- Wake at 10:00 AM; bedtime at 4:00 AM.
- Work starts at 12:00 PM and must include at least 7 hours of work.
- One 1-hour lunch break must be inside the work span and no later than 5:00 PM.
- At least 2 dedicated hours for chores (may be non‑consecutive).
- Hour-by-hour list format. Multi-hour items are written only at the hour they begin; hours with nothing scheduled must be labeled "Free Time".

Hour-by-hour schedule:
- 10:00 AM — Chores (1 hour)
- 11:00 AM — Free Time
- 12:00 PM — Work (start; 3 hours until lunch at 3:00 PM)
- 1:00 PM — See 12:00 PM
- 2:00 PM — See 12:00 PM
- 3:00 PM — Lunch (1 hour; no later than 5:00 PM)
- 4:00 PM — Work (start; 4 hours)
- 5:00 PM — See 4:00 PM
- 6:00 PM — See 4:00 PM
- 7:00 PM — See 4:00 PM
- 8:00 PM — Chores (1 hour)
- 9:00 PM — Free Time
- 10:00 PM — Personal project / Focused hobby (1 hour)
- 11:00 PM — Free Time
- 12:00 AM — Free Time
- 1:00 AM — Free Time
- 2:00 AM — Free Time
- 3:00 AM — Wind-down / pre-bed routine
- 4:00 AM — Bedtime

Notes:
- Work totals 7 hours (12:00–3:00 = 3h, 4:00–8:00 = 4h), with lunch at 3:00 PM (within the work period and before 5:00 PM).
- Chores are at 10:00 AM and 8:00 PM (two dedicated hours).
- Multi-hour blocks are indicated at their start; “See [start time]” marks continuation. If you want the continuing hours labeled differently (e.g., “Work (cont.)”) or want to move chores/lunch to other times, tell me and I’ll adjust.
